schema_name: Human Bond Dynamics – Intervention Layer: Divorce and Grief Retreat Node

source_context.origin_text: En ny affärsidé – att stötta människor i skilsmässa
source_context.conceptual_note: This module formalizes the divorce retreat idea as an intervention layer within HBD rather than as a narrative extension.

core_problem.description: A divorce or severe relationship rupture can remove a person's functional stability, concentration, social confidence and sense of safety. Existing support structures are fragmented and often not available at the acute moment of collapse.
core_problem.structural_gap: No integrated infrastructure exists that combines immediate human reception, safe physical environment, employer legitimacy, professional referral and gradual return-to-function support.
core_problem.critical_observation: The central need is not advice first, but a living human being available when panic peaks.

target_population.need: acute stabilization, confidentiality, temporary withdrawal from pressure, and supported return to work

target_population.group: people in grief or relational loss
target_population.need: safe place, human listening, rest, and access to professional support
a practical way to reduce long-term sickness absence, preserve key personnel and demonstrate genuine human responsibility

trigger_conditions: risk of self-harm or suicidal ideation

signals: need to tell the story repeatedly
need for controlled contact with employer
stabilization_phase.primary_need: human containment and restoration of basic function

intervention_node.name: Divorce and Grief Retreat
intervention_node.function: Provide an immediate, dignified and safe physical environment for people in acute relational or grief-related crisis.
core_functions: employer coordination when authorized by the guest
environmental_design: optional sauna or restorative physical routines

support_roles.description: Receives the guest with calm, dignity and immediate human presence.
Provides non-judgmental human listening; does not diagnose or solve prematurely.
Available for practical life-structure and work reintegration support.
Available for guests who request spiritual or existential support.
Professional operational responsibility for safety, quality and coordination.
Support reception, maintenance, meals, cleaning, laundry and basic care functions.

step_1_pre_registration.description: Employer informs employees that retreat support exists and provides discreet access information.

step_2_crisis_activation.description: Employee contacts retreat during acute crisis, including outside office hours.

step_3_arrival.description: Guest is physically received, fed if needed, given a room and allowed to rest.

step_4_information_and_choice.description: Guest receives clear options: human listener, psychologist, coach, priest/spiritual caregiver, employer contact, or rest only.

step_5_listening_and_stabilization.description: Guest tells the story in a safe context; body and nervous system begin to settle.

step_6_employer_bridge.description: With guest consent, HR contact confirms that staying at the retreat is legitimate and supported.

step_7_gradual_work_reentry.description: Guest may work remotely in limited form if functional capacity returns, without pressure.

step_8_exit_and_aftercare.description: Guest leaves with follow-up options, support contacts and a stabilized short-term plan.

value: reduced risk of long-term sick leave
improved return-to-work pathway after personal crisis
employer_value_proposition.positioning: Not a wellness benefit, but a crisis-stabilization infrastructure.

stigma_risk.problem: If the place is only associated with divorce, it may be stigmatized.
stigma_risk.mitigation: Position as grief retreat or human crisis retreat, open to several forms of loss and acute life disruption.

confidentiality_risk.problem: Guests may fear being recognized or exposed.
confidentiality_risk.mitigation: Strict privacy protocol, no disclosure to employer without explicit consent, neutral naming.

volunteer_quality_risk.problem: Untrained volunteers may provide inconsistent support.
volunteer_quality_risk.mitigation: Clear role boundaries, training, supervision and professional escalation rules.

clinical_risk.problem: Some guests may be suicidal or clinically unstable.
clinical_risk.mitigation: Immediate escalation pathway to healthcare, emergency services and licensed professionals.

implementation_risk.problem: Building physical retreats from the start requires high capital investment.
implementation_risk.mitigation: Pilot with existing retreat/hotel/guesthouse capacity before constructing dedicated facilities.

constraint_layer: human beings cannot always self-regulate under acute relational collapse
work capacity may fail before formal illness is recognized
a safe human container reduces destructive escalation

success_indicators: guest avoids immediate collapse or destructive escalation
guest speaks to another human being
guest avoids unnecessary long-term sick leave
guest maintains limited connection to work when appropriate
guest leaves with a short-term stabilization plan
canonical_statement: Human Bond Dynamics must not only describe relational collapse. It must also define stabilizing nodes where human beings can be held safely when the bond rupture exceeds their self-regulation capacity.

license.ai_training: permitted if attribution is preserved and content remains unaltered
